$comment: Curated compatibility database for known-troublesome Windows apps under Wine, per DESIGN.md §2: 'a compatibility database/launcher (similar in spirit to Lutris' install scripts) curated for your distro specifically, so common known-troublesome apps get auto-applied workarounds on install.' Entries are limited to apps/fixes with well-established, widely-documented behavior (WineHQ AppDB consensus, winetricks' own verb set) -- status 'native-alternative-recommended' apps point at a real native Linux option instead of fabricating a Wine fix that doesn't reliably exist. Loaded by bin/distro-gaming-compat. Each 'winetricks_verbs' entry is a real, current winetricks verb name -- not invented.

apps.name: Microsoft Office 2010/2013/2016 (classic MSI installer, not Click-to-Run/365)
apps.notes: Classic MSI-based Office installs are the long-documented WineHQ AppDB baseline case for this verb set. Click-to-Run (modern 365 installer) and license activation are far less reliable -- don't expect this to cover those.
apps.source: WineHQ AppDB general guidance for the MS Office product line

apps.name: Microsoft Office 365 (Click-to-Run installer)
apps.notes: Click-to-Run's installer and licensing/activation flow are not reliably functional under Wine. Use the classic MSI offline installer if you have access to one (see ms-office-classic), or use Office's web apps (office.com) in a browser, which work natively and fully.
apps.source: Long-standing WineHQ AppDB consensus on Click-to-Run vs classic MSI Office installers

native_alternative.notes: GNOME's native PDF viewer (already pulled in by ubuntu-desktop-minimal/lubuntu-desktop on most strains) handles standard PDFs, forms, and annotations. Reader DC under Wine is a known-poor experience (installer issues, plugin/update-service crashes) with no reliable fix.

apps.name: Adobe Creative Cloud desktop apps (Photoshop CC, Illustrator CC, Premiere/After Effects)
apps.notes: The Creative Cloud installer/licensing service and the apps themselves are not reliably functional under Wine -- this is the same DRM/licensing-service problem flagged in DESIGN.md §2/§4 for Premiere/After Effects. Don't attempt; see modes/creative/README.md for the native alternatives Refract OS actually ships (FreeCAD, Blender, DaVinci Resolve, Kdenlive).

apps.name: Apple iTunes
apps.notes: Long-standing, never-fully-resolved WineHQ AppDB case -- installer/service crashes, Apple Mobile Device Support failures. No reliable workaround exists; there's also no good native-Linux equivalent for iOS device sync specifically. If only used for music playback, a native player (Rhythmbox/Clementine) plus moving the music library off-device is the realistic path, not a Wine fix.

apps.name: Epic Games Launcher

native_alternative.notes: Heroic Games Launcher is a native, actively-maintained open-source client for Epic (and GOG/Amazon) game libraries -- a far more reliable path than running Epic's own launcher under Wine, which has long-standing UI rendering and login issues.

apps.source: Heroic Games Launcher project (heroicgameslauncher.com); widely known as the standard Linux alternative to Epic's launcher

native_alternative.notes: Heroic Games Launcher also covers GOG library management/installs natively. GOG itself also sells DRM-free installers that work fine standalone without Galaxy at all.

apps.name: Battle.net launcher (Blizzard)
apps.notes: Use Lutris' own maintained Battle.net install script rather than a hand-rolled prefix -- Lutris tracks Blizzard's frequent launcher updates more closely than a static verb list here ever could. This entry exists so the database is honest about the recommended path (defer to Lutris) instead of pretending to have a static fix.
apps.source: Lutris install-script database (lutris.net) maintains the current Battle.net recipe

apps.name: Discord

native_alternative.notes: Discord ships an official native Linux client (also available as a .deb from discord.com or via Flathub). No reason to run the Windows build under Wine.

apps.source: Discord's own official Linux downloads page

apps.name: Spotify

native_alternative.notes: Spotify ships an official native Linux client (Snap, or Flathub). No reason to run the Windows build under Wine.

apps.source: Spotify's own official Linux downloads page

apps.name: Generic .NET Framework desktop app (no native Linux build)
apps.notes: Not a specific app -- a generic starting point for the very common 'this one app needs .NET and a specific font' case DESIGN.md §2 calls out winetricks as the answer for. Try this verb set first for unlisted .NET-based Windows utilities before assuming Wine can't run it at all.

apps.name: Notepad++
apps.notes: Rates Platinum/Gold on WineHQ AppDB and runs near-flawlessly out of the box; corefonts is cosmetic (cleaner font rendering), not required to launch. Plugins generally work. Native-feeling alternatives if preferred: Geany (apt: geany) or VSCodium (flatpak: com.vscodium.codium).

apps.name: IrfanView
apps.notes: Platinum/Gold on WineHQ AppDB; the 64-bit build runs well out of the box, corefonts just tidies UI fonts. Optionally add gdiplus and the PlugIns pack. Native alternatives: nomacs (apt: nomacs) or gThumb (apt: gthumb).

apps.name: foobar2000
apps.notes: Runs well under Wine and most plugins work (see github.com/dweymouth/foobar-on-linux for .desktop/MIME integration); ensure a working Wine audio backend (winepulse/pipewire, default in modern Wine). If a Wine regression causes crashes, pin a known-good Wine branch. Native alternatives with a similar philosophy: DeaDBeeF (flatpak: music.deadbeef.player) or Strawberry (flatpak: org.strawberrymusicplayer.strawberry).

apps.name: Adobe Photoshop CS6 (13.0)
apps.notes: Use a WINEARCH=win32 prefix. CS6 is the last well-supported Photoshop under Wine (long-standing Gold-ish AppDB consensus); the subscription CC versions are far harder/broken. For new work, native GIMP (flatpak: org.gimp.GIMP) or Krita (flatpak: org.kde.krita) are the real answers.
apps.source: WineHQ AppDB + community CS6-on-Wine verb recipes

apps.name: 7-Zip

native_alternative.notes: p7zip is the same 7-Zip engine natively (apt: p7zip-full, plus p7zip-rar for RAR); for a 7-Zip-style GUI use PeaZip (Flathub: io.github.peazip.PeaZip), which uses the p7zip backend. The Windows 7-Zip GUI runs Platinum under Wine but there's no reason to use it.

apps.notes: Archiver — use the native engine, not Wine.

apps.name: WinRAR

native_alternative.notes: unrar (apt, multiverse) extracts RARs; RARLAB also ships official native Linux 'rar'/'unrar' binaries (win-rar.com/rar-linux-mac.html) — the 'rar' binary is needed to CREATE rar archives. PeaZip gives a GUI.

apps.notes: RARLAB ships native Linux binaries, so Wine is unnecessary.

apps.name: Microsoft Teams

native_alternative.notes: Microsoft discontinued the native Linux Teams client and the Windows client doesn't work reliably under Wine. Official path is the Teams PWA in a browser; for a standalone app with tray/notifications use teams-for-linux (Flathub: com.github.IsmaelMartinez.teams_for_linux), the de-facto community wrapper.

apps.notes: No working Wine path; use the PWA or teams-for-linux.

apps.name: MSI Afterburner

native_alternative.notes: Kernel-level GPU tools can't work under Wine (they need the RTCore driver + direct hardware access). Native replacements: CoreCtrl (apt: corectrl) for AMD; GreenWithEnvy (Flathub: com.leinardi.gwe) for NVIDIA; MangoHud (apt: mangohud) for the RTSS-style on-screen overlay.

apps.notes: Categorically broken under Wine (kernel-mode driver); use native GPU tools.

apps.name: Rufus

native_alternative.notes: Rufus needs raw physical-disk access Wine doesn't pass through, so it can't write bootable USBs under Wine. Native: balenaEtcher (Flathub: io.balena.etcher), GNOME Disks 'Restore Disk Image' (apt: gnome-disk-utility), Ventoy (ventoy.net), or plain dd.

apps.notes: Needs direct device access; broken under Wine.

apps.name: Paint.NET

native_alternative.notes: Modern Paint.NET depends on Direct2D + the Windows Animation Manager, both severely underdeveloped in Wine — it doesn't run reliably. Pinta (Flathub: com.github.PintaProject.Pinta) is the closest Paint.NET-style layered editor; Krita (apt: krita / flathub: org.kde.krita) for richer work.

apps.notes: Broken under Wine (Direct2D); use Pinta/Krita.

apps.name: SketchUp (desktop)

native_alternative.notes: Desktop SketchUp 2017+ fails under Wine with OpenGL init errors ('ChoosePixelFormat failed') — Wine's GL translation doesn't satisfy its OpenGL 3.x needs. Supported cross-platform path is the SketchUp Free WEB app (app.sketchup.com); native desktop CAD: FreeCAD (flathub: org.freecad.FreeCAD) or Blender (flathub: org.blender.Blender).

apps.notes: Broken under Wine (OpenGL); use the web app or FreeCAD/Blender.

apps.name: Roblox

native_alternative.notes: Roblox's Hyperion/Byfron anticheat intentionally blocks Wine, so the Windows client + Grapejuice/Vinegar Wine path is dead. Sober (Flathub: org.vinegarhq.Sober) runs the Roblox ANDROID client containerized on Linux (NOT Wine). Unofficial, x86_64 + SSE4.2 only, and may be blocked by Roblox at any time — use at your own account risk.

apps.notes: Pure-Wine Roblox is broken by anticheat; Sober is the non-Wine community path.

apps.name: Affinity Photo / Designer / Publisher (Serif)
apps.notes: Does NOT work on stock Wine/Proton. It runs only via Lutris using the ElementalWarrior custom Wine fork plus specific tricks (vcrun2022, dotnet48, corefonts, tahoma, a Windows.winmd + wintypes shim, DXVK/Vulkan renderer) — fiddly and fork-dependent, not a plain-winetricks workaround, so it's listed as broken-for-mainline-Wine. If you need it, follow the AffinityOnLinux Lutris install script (github.com/seapear/AffinityOnLinux). Native alternatives for new work: GIMP/Krita (raster), Inkscape (apt: inkscape) (vector), Scribus (apt: scribus) (layout).

apps.name: Kernel-anticheat games (Valorant, Fortnite, PUBG, CoD, etc.)
apps.notes: Titles whose kernel-level anticheat is NOT enabled for Linux by the developer (Vanguard, or EAC/BattlEye without Proton enablement) will NOT run, and attempting them can flag/ban the account. No winetricks verb fixes anticheat. Some EAC/BattlEye games DO work when the dev ships Proton/Linux support — always check the specific title on areweanticheatyet.com / ProtonDB before assuming. (Genshin Impact runs technically via Proton but is officially unsupported with non-zero ban risk — use at your own risk.)
